title: Global Project Portfolio Governance & PMO Leader
required_skills: 10+ years in portfolio management, PMO, transformation, or operations
Strong understanding of manufacturing, supply chain, quality, regulatory, MSAT
Portfolio governance model design (intake, prioritisation, milestone tracking)
Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred
matched_skills: Portfolio governance fundamentals: HSBC 6-workstream programme governance, risk escalation, prioritisation decisions
Executive-level reporting: C-suite budget variance dashboards and burn-rate reports at HSBC
Risk and dependency management: programme status and risk escalation across 6 workstreams at HSBC
KPI framework and reporting: KPI dashboards, Power BI PL-300 certified
Cross-functional coordination: Product, Engineering, Marketing, C-suite at HSBC; Hapag-Lloyd agile coordination
Stakeholder communications: data storytelling for investment decisions
missing_skills: 10+ years of experience (candidate has ~4 years total — major seniority gap)
Manufacturing/supply chain/quality/regulatory/MSAT domain expertise (no operations background)
Portfolio governance model design at enterprise scale (HSBC was 6-workstream, not full enterprise portfolio)
PMO team leadership and capability building (candidate has been individual contributor/coordinator)
PMP or equivalent formal project management certification
US-based or US-market role (candidate is Germany-based)
core_responsibilities: Design and maintain portfolio governance models, standards, and operating rhythms
Lead enterprise portfolio governance and PMO operating models
Translate enterprise strategy into actionable, prioritised roadmaps
Provide executive-level execution oversight, own portfolio reporting
Track initiative health, milestones, risks, dependencies
Develop dashboards, scorecards, and executive summaries
recommended_emphasis: This role targets a senior leader with 10+ years and domain expertise the candidate lacks — application is a stretch
If applying, lead with HSBC USD 8M programme governance as the strongest evidence of PMO fundamentals
Highlight Power BI PL-300 certification and dashboard/scorecard experience as matching the reporting and insights requirement
Be transparent about years of experience gap; position as an emerging PMO professional rather than a senior leader
summary: Match score 47.4% — weak match. The role is designed for a senior PMO leader with 10+ years and manufacturing/supply chain domain expertise, both of which represent hard gaps for this candidate with ~4 years and no operations background. The HSBC programme governance experience provides genuine PMO fundamentals, but the seniority requirement and domain specialisation make this a significant stretch. Application is only recommended if the candidate is comfortable with a likely screen-out at the experience level filter.
